title: Colymbada scopulorum
taxonomy: Colymbada scopulorum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe of the sunflower family, Asteraceae. It is a member of the genus Colymbada, which is a group of plants that were previously classified as part of the genus Anthemis. Colymbada scopulorum is native to the southwestern United States, specifically in the mountainous regions of New Mexico and Colorado. The plant is characterized by its yellow or white flowers, which are typically small and clustered together. Colymbada scopulorum is a relatively rare and local species, and its classification is closely tied to its unique morphology and geography.

intro: Colymbada scopulorum, also known as Alpine Saw-wort, is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant has been found to possess several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can be beneficial in treating various ailments such as wounds, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Colymbada scopulorum include flavonoids, sesquiterpene lactones, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
